Military Apr 4, 2026

Why The C-5M Super Galaxy's 28 Wheels Must Rotate 90 Degrees Just To Retract

The brilliant engineering solution enables a massive payload and efficient flight performance.

The C-5M's unique 90-degree wheel rotation during retraction exemplifies how military cargo aircraft must compromise between maximum payload capacity and aerodynamic efficiency through innovative landing gear design.

See Simple Flying →

Military Apr 4, 2026

Iran Attained A Fleet Of F-14s Even Though The US Regretted It – Now They’re Destroyed

During the Cold War, Iran was viewed as a crucial stop-block for Soviet southern expansion, the F-14 played a role in this effort.

Iran's operational F-14 fleet, a legacy of Cold War geopolitics, represented a significant regional air power asset that influenced Middle Eastern military balance for decades.

See Simple Flying →

How to Assess Safety Risk

A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.

Part 5's safety risk assessment framework provides systematic methodology for identifying hazards before incidents occur, shifting focus from reactive to proactive risk management.

Commercial Apr 4, 2026

The 7 Longest Boeing 787 Dreamliner Routes In The World

Discover the seven longest Boeing 787 flights of 2026 and the airlines that make them possible.

Extended 787 ultra-long-haul deployments reveal airlines' confidence in the aircraft's efficiency for point-to-point routes that bypass traditional hub-and-spoke networks.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 4, 2026

Why Hawaiian Airlines Pilots Must Shave Their Beards During Alaska Integration

Hawaiian pilots will no longer be allowed beards under new Alaska uniform standards.

Grooming policy harmonization during airline mergers can create workforce friction, requiring careful change management to maintain pilot morale during integration.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 4, 2026

Artemis 2 in good shape cruising towards the moon

A day after lighting its engine to head to the moon, the Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft is performing well with only minor issues.

Minor thermal and propulsion anomalies detected during Artemis 2's trans-lunar injection are manageable, reducing mission risk and supporting NASA's crewed lunar landing timeline.

See SpaceNews →

Regulatory Apr 4, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

Operators must formally document their safety management systems meet regulatory standards, with the FAA conducting audits to verify compliance beyond initial certification.

Regulatory Apr 4, 2026

Trump budget seeks $372 million cut to US Essential Air Service program

US President Donald Trump’s fiscal 2027 budget proposal takes aim at the Essential Air Service program, calling for…...

Eliminating EAS subsidies could force 40+ rural airports to lose commercial service, disrupting communities with limited transportation alternatives and increasing operating costs for regional carriers.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Apr 4, 2026

What It’s Like Flying In A Military Transport Aircraft

Uncle Sam's no-frills flying experience.

Military transport operations prioritize mission capability and efficiency over passenger comfort, offering commercial aviation insights into cost optimization and operational discipline.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

What United Airlines' New Relax Row Economy Beds Will Likely Cost On Long-Haul Flights

These beds could come with an appealing price tag.

United's economy bed product positioning at premium pricing could shift long-haul seat inventory strategy and capture price-sensitive customers willing to pay above standard economy fares.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Apr 4, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 departure from RNO's parallel runways 17L/R requires strict adherence to specific procedures that pilots frequently overlook, creating potential safety risks in high-altitude terrain operations.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

NASA Sets Coverage for Northrop Grumman’s CRS-24 Resupply Launch

NASA, Northrop Grumman, and SpaceX are targeting no earlier than 8:49 a.m. EDT on Wednesday, April 8, for the next launch delivering science investigations, supplies, and equipment to the International Space Station. Filled with approximately 11,000 pounds of cargo, the Northrop Grumman Cygnus XL...

Northrop Grumman's larger Cygnus XL variant increases ISS resupply capacity by delivering 11,000 pounds of cargo, expanding commercial logistics capabilities for space stations.

See NASA →

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

6 Airlines With The World’s Longest Economy Seat Pitch

Which airlines are currently offering the most seat pitch for their respective economy class products? Here are 6 airlines that have mastered space.

Airlines competing on seat pitch as a differentiation factor signals economy-class comfort is becoming a competitive battleground as carriers seek non-price advantages.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 3, 2026

Why Do Airlines Still Use 3-3 Seating On Narrowbody Jets?

Narrowbody aircraft are limited by regulations to max out at 3-3 seating, while changing their fundamental design means they are not narrowbodies.

Airlines accept 3-3 seating constraints because redesigning fuselages for higher-density layouts would reclassify aircraft and trigger costlier regulatory compliance requirements.

See Simple Flying →

▶
Safety Apr 4, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strike fatalities persist because pilots systematically underestimate reaction time constraints, making pre-flight route planning and low-altitude awareness critical operational safeguards.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Military Apr 3, 2026

US A-10 hit during Iran rescue mission, pilot ejects and is rescued

A US Air Force A-10 was hit during combat operations tied to the widening war with Iran on…...

Combat losses in active conflict zones underscore the continued vulnerability of legacy aircraft platforms and the critical importance of modern air defense systems and pilot recovery capabilities.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Apr 3, 2026

The World’s Largest Air Forces By Annual Budget

A closer look at the world leaders in air power spending.

Global air force spending concentration reveals strategic regional power dynamics, with top spenders investing heavily in modernization and next-generation capabilities to maintain competitive advantages.

Military Apr 3, 2026

US Air Force A-10 Thunderbolt II Crashes Amid Search For Downed F-15 Eagle

The pilot was successfully rescued.

A-10 loss during combat search-and-rescue operations underscores operational risks when deploying legacy platforms in contested airspace environments.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Apr 4, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-airspace systems offers faster environmental gains than aircraft technology alone, requiring coordinated investment in air traffic management and ground infrastructure.

See MDPI →

Regulatory Apr 3, 2026

Michael Graham Named NTSB Vice Chairman

The certified transport pilot and NTSB board member will co-head the agency for a three-year term.

Graham's transport pilot background brings operational expertise to NTSB leadership, enhancing the agency's credibility in accident investigation and safety recommendations.

See AVweb →

Regulatory Apr 3, 2026

US and Europe revise GPS interference guidelines as spoofing risks grow

Aviation authorities are warning that instances of GPS interference have spread across a widening range of high-risk airspace,…...

Growing GPS spoofing incidents across critical airspace are forcing regulators to strengthen backup navigation protocols, raising compliance costs for operators.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Apr 3, 2026

A-10 Thunderbolt II Crashed Near the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Officials Tell NYT

Almost the same time as an F-15E Strike Eagle went down over Iran, the U.S. Air Force lost an A-10 Thunderbolt II attack aircraft near the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. officials told NYT that the jet’s pilot was safely rescued. While search and rescue efforts for at least one of the crew members of an […]...

Dual U.S. military aircraft losses in the Middle East within hours suggests heightened operational tempo or conflict escalation in a strategically critical region.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Meet NASA’s New Artemis II Science Officers

NASA’s first Artemis II science officers—Kelsey Young, Trevor Graff, and Angela Garcia—are pioneering a new era of mission operations by integrating advanced lunar science directly into Mission Control. Their work will shape how future Artemis crews observe, analyze, and deepen our understanding of...

Embedding dedicated science officers in Mission Control represents a fundamental shift from traditional astronaut-centric operations to real-time scientific decision-making that will accelerate lunar exploration capabilities.

See NASA →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Italy’s Argotec plans to scale Florida satellite facility to meet rising US demand

Italy’s Argotec has officially opened its first U.S. satellite production facility, cementing a foothold near Kennedy Space Center in Florida to join other foreign space firms pursuing growing demand from American programs.

Foreign satellite manufacturers are establishing U.S. production bases to capture domestic demand from government and commercial space programs, signaling intensified competition in the American aerospace supply chain.

See SpaceNews →

Military Apr 3, 2026

US F-15E Shot Down Over Iran, One Crew Member Rescued

The fate of the aircraft’s second crew member is unknown.

Loss of advanced fighter aircraft over hostile territory demonstrates persistent air defense threats and operational risks in contested airspace requiring enhanced pilot recovery protocols.

See AVweb →

Regulatory Apr 3, 2026

FAA report proposes sweeping rewrite of Part 141 flight training rules

A 471-page industry report released through the FAA’s Part 141 modernization docket proposes a sweeping rewrite of how…...

The comprehensive Part 141 rewrite signals the FAA's recognition that current regulations, largely unchanged since 1988, no longer reflect modern aircraft technology and training methodologies.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Virgil I. “Gus” Grissom

Today marks the 100th anniversary of the birth of Virgil I. “Gus” Grissom, born April 3, 1926, in Mitchell, Indiana. As one of NASA’s first seven astronauts, he became America’s second astronaut to fly in space when he launched aboard the Liberty Bell 7 spacecraft on July 21, 1961, just weeks after...

Grissom's pioneering Mercury missions established critical protocols for crewed spaceflight that remain foundational to modern aerospace operations and human spaceflight safety standards.

See NASA →

Military Apr 3, 2026

Picture of the Day: April 3, 2026

A C-130J awaits its next assignment on the flightline.

The C-130J's sustained operational readiness demonstrates the critical importance of maintenance infrastructure and ground support coordination in military airlift missions.

See AVweb →

Regulatory Apr 3, 2026

ZeroAvia edges closer to cert as FAA issues electric engine conditions

ZeroAvia has this week celebrated a “major step” towards certification of its ZA600 hydrogen-electric powertrain, after the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) published special conditions to certify the system’s electric engine component. The FAA published 33 special conditions for...

ZeroAvia's FAA special conditions for electric engines establish regulatory precedent that will accelerate certification pathways for other hydrogen-electric propulsion developers.

See Runway Girl Network →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

White House again proposes steep NASA budget cuts

For the second consecutive year, the White House is proposing a major budget cut for NASA that would significantly impact the agency’s science programs and the International Space Station.

Sustained NASA budget cuts threaten ISS operations and space-based Earth observation capabilities critical for aviation weather forecasting and climate monitoring.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Test Pilots and the Road Back to the Moon

For NASA’s first crewed lunar mission since Apollo, aviation experience is one of the clearest threads linking the astronauts aboard Orion.

NASA's Artemis lunar program prioritizes recruiting test pilots whose deep experience with experimental aircraft directly translates to managing the unprecedented risks of returning humans to the Moon.

See AVweb →

Drones Apr 3, 2026

New DJI Terra update adds HEIF support, boosts reconstruction performance

DJI is continuing to refine its mapping and modeling ecosystem with the latest update to DJI Terra, and version 5.2.0 brings a mix of speed boosts, smarter automation, and improved output quality for enterprise users. more…...

DJI Terra's performance improvements and HEIF support enable faster, higher-quality mapping workflows that could reduce operational costs for enterprise drone operators in surveying and infrastructure sectors.

See DroneDJ →

Aerospace Apr 3, 2026

Hello, World

NASA astronaut and Artemis II Commander Reid Wiseman took this picture of Earth from the Orion spacecraft’s window after completing the translunar injection burn. There are two auroras (top right and bottom left) and zodiacal light (bottom right) is visible as the Earth eclipses the Sun. This and...

Artemis II's translunar trajectory enables unprecedented Earth observation opportunities that advance both human spaceflight operations and atmospheric science capabilities.

See NASA →

Military Apr 3, 2026

US rescues one airman after F-15E shot down over Iran, search continues for second

Unverified images circulating on social media on April 3, 2026, appear to show the wreckage of a US…...

Loss of advanced F-15E aircraft over hostile territory raises critical questions about air superiority assumptions and aircraft survivability in modern air defense environments.

Drones Apr 3, 2026

US drone future hinges on policy, investment, coordination

The Commercial Drone Alliance is pushing Washington to move faster, and more strategically, on building a truly domestic drone ecosystem. In a newly released white paper, the industry group calls on the Trump Administration to adopt sweeping reforms aimed at strengthening US manufacturing, creating...

Industry leaders are demanding coordinated federal strategy to compete with China's drone dominance, signaling that regulatory clarity alone won't secure US market leadership without manufacturing investment.
